query:
get_embedding exists at line 312. One important detail
  before the full answers:                                
                                                          
  OQ-5: It iterates all HNSW layers to find the point by  
  origin_id — O(N) per call, no batch API. At current     
  corpus size this is fine, but the tick should select    
  source candidates first, then call get_embedding only   
  for those — not for all entries. The throttle on        
  max_supports_per_tick bounds pairs, not source-embedding
   lookups, so keep candidate selection tight.

  ---
  OQ-1 (tick interval) — every tick.
                                                          
  The max_supports_per_tick throttle is the right control.
   An additional interval parameter is a second knob for  
  the same concern. The graph starts empty and needs to 
  fill; running every tick gets you there faster. If tick 
  time becomes a problem, add the interval then. Don't add
   it upfront.

  OQ-2 (Prerequisite scope) — defer to W3-1.

  Asymmetric NLI entailment is a weak proxy for           
  "prerequisite." It captures directionality of support,
  not topic ordering. Without a domain-anchored "category 
  A is prerequisite-of category B" signal, you're writing
  noisy Prerequisite edges that PPR will traverse as
  positive signal — which could hurt ranking. PPR is gated
   on Supports + CoAccess edges being in place;
  Prerequisite is additive. Ship #412 with Supports only,
  keep the scope clean.

  OQ-3 (K neighbours config) — add graph_inference_k.

  nli_post_store_k is a hot-path knob (post-store         
  contradiction check, latency-sensitive). Graph inference
   is a background tick with no latency budget. They have 
  different semantics and could reasonably want different
  values. Sharing the knob creates invisible coupling —
  tuning one breaks the other. Default graph_inference_k
  to the same value initially, but keep them separate in
  InferenceConfig.

  OQ-4 (pair deduplication) — pre-filter.

  NLI is the expensive operation. As the graph fills over 
  sessions, the fraction of candidate pairs that already
  have a Supports edge grows monotonically. Spending NLI  
  budget re-confirming existing edges means fewer new   
  pairs get processed per tick. One indexed DB read per
  pair is negligible. Pre-filter strictly dominates INSERT
   OR IGNORE over time — the gap widens as the graph
  matures.

entries.title: ADR-003 crt-029: Source-Candidate Bound Derived from max_graph_inference_per_tick, No Separate Config Field
Tick passes over full entry set: bound, shuffle, and embedding-filter source candidates independently of NLI pair cap
ADR-004 crt-034: max_co_access_promotion_per_tick Added to InferenceConfig
ADR-004 crt-023: Post-Store Embedding Handoff — Move Vec<f32> Into Fire-and-Forget Task
Graph inference edge stall: supports_edge_threshold too conservative for retrospective-dominated corpus
